name: Saylani Welfare Trust Usa
mission: Saylani Welfare International Trust has been working since 1999 to improve the conditions of the less privileged, helpless, and handicapped individuals ...
theoryOfChange: Combining immediate relief (food/medical) with long-term empowerment (IT training/education) to break the cycle of poverty.
scoreSummary: Saylani Welfare Trust USA shows strong alignment and good impact, with zakat compliance (minor risk deductions applied).
zakatClaimEvidence: On the 'Donations' page of Saylani Welfare Trust - USA, 'Sadqah & Zakat' is listed as a donation option, and there is a 'Sadqa Zakat' quick donate option. Additionally, the FAQ section on the same page includes the question, 'Can I pay my zakat donation or only sadqa (non-zakat) donaitons are accepted?' (Source: https://saylaniwelfareusa.com/)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

dimension_explanations.alignment: With an alignment score of 39/50, the charity shows high donor fit for those seeking humanitarian causes and Zakat-eligible opportunities [1][3]. The mission focuses on urgent humanitarian needs, including aid for the handicapped and helpless, which aligns with high-priority charitable giving [2].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization operates a direct service model with a total revenue of $2,954,546, maintaining a high program expense ratio of 98.8% to support its feeding and medical initiatives [1][2]. While the directness of service is high, the impact score of 29/50 reflects a need for more comprehensive evidence regarding cost-per-beneficiary metrics [1].

baseline_narrative.headline: Saylani Welfare Trust USA provides critical humanitarian relief and food security to vulnerable populations with exceptional financial efficiency.
strengths: Exceptional program expense ratio of 98.8%, ensuring nearly all funds reach those in need
High fundraising efficiency with a cost of $0.00 per $1 raised
baseline_narrative.summary: Established as part of a mission starting in 1999, Saylani Welfare Trust USA focuses on improving conditions for the less privileged through medical support and free meal programs like Saylani Dastarkhwan [1][2]. The organization is recognized as Zakat-eligible, allowing donors to fulfill their religious obligations through various giving methods [3].

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who prioritize maximum financial efficiency and want their zakat to fund direct humanitarian relief in Pakistan through an established mission.
donor_motivations: Want 100% of their donation to reach program services
Seek to fulfill zakat obligations through verified asnaf categories
Value high-growth organizations with low overhead
Focus on immediate relief like food and medical aid
giving_considerations: The organization has very low financial reserves
Impact data is still developing regarding long-term outcomes
Focus is geographically concentrated in Pakistan
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking organizations with large financial endowments or those requiring multi-year longitudinal impact studies.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects verified outcomes and a high program ratio, but notes the need for more consistent beneficiary reporting.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: The charity provides immediate relief (food, water, medicine) to alleviate poverty, though the long-term causal pathway to self-sufficiency is not explicitly documented.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Consistent impact data ensures that the 100% program spend is translating into the highest possible benefit for the poor.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With a massive revenue growth rate and low reserves, the charity can absorb significant capital to stabilize and expand.

strengths.detail: The organization directs an <cite id="1">exceptional 100.0% of its expenses toward programs</cite>, significantly exceeding the <cite id="4">peer program ratio median of 90.3%</cite>.
The charity demonstrates elite fiscal management with a <cite id="1">fundraising efficiency of $0.00 per $1 raised</cite>.
The organization holds a <cite id="1">90.5/100 overall score from Charity Navigator</cite> and has earned a Gold Seal of Transparency from Candid.

strengths_deep_dive: Saylani Welfare Trust USA exhibits a rare level of financial leaness, reporting a <cite id="2">98.8% program expense ratio</cite> in its most recent filings. This efficiency is supported by its <cite id="1">Accountability & Finance beacon score of 90</cite> and a perfect <cite id="1">Culture & Community score of 100</cite>. The organization's ability to scale is evident in its <cite id="4">revenue growth from $126,706 in 2022 to over $1.7 million in 2023</cite>, while maintaining its commitment to direct service delivery in <cite id="5">high-need areas like food security and medical support</cite>.
rich_narrative.summary: Saylani Welfare Trust USA operates as a critical humanitarian bridge, providing essential services to vulnerable populations in Pakistan. The organization is part of a broader mission that has been <cite id="1">operating since 2022 to improve conditions</cite> for the less privileged, handicapped, and helpless. In the 2023 tax year, the charity reported <cite id="2">total revenue of $1,727,397</cite>, representing a significant <cite id="4">3-year revenue CAGR of 1263.3%</cite>. This rapid growth is matched by an <cite id="1">exceptional program expense ratio of 98.8%</cite>, indicating that every dollar of public support is directed toward its core missions. These missions include the <cite id="5">Saylani Dastarkhwan free meal program</cite>, medical support, and clean water initiatives. The organization maintains a <cite id="1">90.5/100 overall score from Charity Navigator</cite>, bolstered by a <cite id="1">fundraising efficiency of $0.00 per $1 raised</cite>. As a <cite id="3">zakat-eligible entity</cite>, it provides a verified channel for Muslim donors to fulfill religious obligations through direct service delivery to the <cite id="6">fuqara and masakin</cite>.
